CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant The State Bar Of California Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Following Grant Of Special Motion To Strike Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sean T. Strauss, esq. (415-538-2369) of the office of General Counsel for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued, counsel presents a proposed order. The Court instructs counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Attorney's Fees Following Grant Of Special Motion To Strike.

Defendant State Bar of California's motion for an award of attorney's fees pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(c) is granted in the amount of $28,400. No opposition filed.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig K. Martin, appearing on his own behalf as plaintiff, Telephone 415-642-4803;
Sean T. Strauss, Esq., Assistant General Counsel, The State Bar of California, appearing for Defendant;
No other appearance.

Ruling - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant The State Bar of California's motion to strike portions of Plaintiff Craig K. Martin's complaint is off calendar as moot per the court's ruling on the Anti-SLAPP motion.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Appearances:
Craig K. Martin, appearing on his own behalf as plaintiff, Telephone 415-642-4803;
Sean T. Strauss, Esq., Assistant General Counsel, The State Bar of California, appearing for Defendant;

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Verified Complaint For Damages
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig K. Martin, appearing on his own behalf as plaintiff, Telephone 415-642-4803;
Sean T. Strauss, Esq., Assistant General Counsel, The State Bar of California, appearing for Defendant;
No other appearance.

Ruling - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Verified Complaint For Damages.

Defendant The State Bar of California's special motion to strike Plaintiff Craig K. Martin's complaint is granted. Defendant prevails on both prongs of the anti-SLAPP motion analysis. First, Defendant has shown that the course of action challenged by Plaintiff arises from protected petitioning or free speech activity. As Plaintiff concedes in his opposition, his claims arise entirely from the allegedly false statement about his failure to pass the California Professional Responsibility Exam (CPRE) by February 15, 1993 as required by the State Bar and the resulting suspension of his legal license. (See, e.g., Verified Complaint 5, 6, 43, 48, 54, 60, 67, 74, 80, 82, 84.) The publication of Plaintiff's suspension from legal practice on April 23, 1993 on the online State Bar Profile constitutes a "writing made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by a . . . judicial body, or any other official proceeding authorized by law" because it related to the judicial proceedings of the State Bar Court in issuing Plaintiff's suspension. (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(e)(2); see also Barry v. State Bar of California (2017) 2 Cal.5th 318, 322 [detailing reasoning of trial court that State Bar Court proceedings arise from protected petitioning activities for purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute].)

Regarding the second prong of the anti-SLAPP test, Plaintiff has not established a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits of his claims as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(b)(1), (2). As an initial matter, Plaintiff has failed to put forth any evidence in support of his allegation that the information published on the online State Bar Profile is false and defamatory. He has therefore failed to meet his burden on proof on the second prong. Moreover, Defendant has provided ample evidence to show that no false or defamatory statement was published on Plaintiff's State Bar Profile. Finally, Plaintiff cannot show a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits of his claims because he previously brought a substantially identical claim, which Court dismissed by order dated December 9, 2015 granting defendants' special motion to strike; that order is res judicata. Accordingly, Plaintiff's complaint is dismissed with prejudice as to all Defendants. Defendant The State Bar of California is granted leave to file a motion for attorneys' fees and costs incurred in defense of Plaintiff's lawsuit per Code of Civil Procedure 425.16(c).

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
